See the [command-line overrides section](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-overrides) for more information.

[`-C` _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-update--C)

Changes the current working directory before executing any specified operations. This affects
things like where cargo looks by default for the project manifest (`Cargo.toml`), as well as
the directories searched for discovering `.cargo/config.toml`, for example. This option must
appear before the command name, for example `cargo -C path/to/my-project build`.

This option is only available on the [nightly\\
channel](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/appendix-07-nightly-rust.html) and
requires the `-Z unstable-options` flag to enable (see
[#10098](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/10098)).

[`-h`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-update--h)[`--help`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-update---help)

Prints help information.

[`-Z` _flag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-update--Z)

Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo. Run `cargo -Z help` for details.

## ENVIRONMENT

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#environment-variables-1) for
details on environment variables that Cargo reads.

## EXIT STATUS

- `0`: Cargo succeeded.
- `101`: Cargo failed to complete.

## EXAMPLES

1. Update all dependencies in the lockfile:


```

cargo update
```

2. Update only specific dependencies:


```

cargo update foo bar
```

3. Set a specific dependency to a specific version:


```

cargo update foo --precise 1.2.3
```


## SEE ALSO

[cargo(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo1), [cargo-generate-lockfile(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-generate-lockfile1)

# cargo-vendor(1)

## NAME

cargo-vendor — Vendor all dependencies locally

## SYNOPSIS

`cargo vendor` \[ _options_\] \[ _path_\]

## DESCRIPTION

This cargo subcommand will vendor all crates.io and git dependencies for a
project into the specified directory at `<path>`. After this command completes
the vendor directory specified by `<path>` will contain all remote sources from
dependencies specified. Additional manifests beyond the default one can be
specified with the `-s` option.

The configuration necessary to use the vendored sources would be printed to
stdout after `cargo vendor` completes the vendoring process.
You will need to add or redirect it to your Cargo configuration file,
which is usually `.cargo/config.toml` locally for the current package.

Cargo treats vendored sources as read-only as it does to registry and git sources.
If you intend to modify a crate from a remote source,
use `[patch]` or a `path` dependency pointing to a local copy of that crate.
Cargo will then correctly handle the crate on incremental rebuilds,
as it knows that it is no longer a read-only dependency.

## OPTIONS

### Vendor Options

[`-s` _manifest_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor--s)[`--sync` _manifest_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---sync)

Specify an extra `Cargo.toml` manifest to workspaces which should also be
vendored and synced to the output. May be specified multiple times.

[`--no-delete`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---no-delete)

Don’t delete the “vendor” directory when vendoring, but rather keep all
existing contents of the vendor directory

[`--respect-source-config`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---respect-source-config)

Instead of ignoring `[source]` configuration by default in `.cargo/config.toml`
read it and use it when downloading crates from crates.io, for example

[`--versioned-dirs`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---versioned-dirs)

Normally versions are only added to disambiguate multiple versions of the
same package. This option causes all directories in the “vendor” directory
to be versioned, which makes it easier to track the history of vendored
packages over time, and can help with the performance of re-vendoring when
only a subset of the packages have changed.

### Manifest Options

[`--manifest-path` _path_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---manifest-path)

Path to the `Cargo.toml` file. By default, Cargo searches for the
`Cargo.toml` file in the current directory or any parent directory.

[`--locked`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---locked)

Asserts that the exact same dependencies and versions are used as when the
existing `Cargo.lock` file was originally generated. Cargo will exit with an
error when either of the following scenarios arises:

- The lock file is missing.
- Cargo attempted to change the lock file due to a different dependency resolution.

It may be used in environments where deterministic builds are desired,
such as in CI pipelines.

[`--offline`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---offline)

Prevents Cargo from accessing the network for any reason. Without this
flag, Cargo will stop with an error if it needs to access the network and
the network is not available. With this flag, Cargo will attempt to
proceed without the network if possible.

Beware that this may result in different dependency resolution than online
mode. Cargo will restrict itself to crates that are downloaded locally, even
if there might be a newer version as indicated in the local copy of the index.
See the [cargo-fetch(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-fetch1) command to download dependencies before going
offline.

May also be specified with the `net.offline` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--frozen`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---frozen)

Equivalent to specifying both `--locked` and `--offline`.

### Display Options

[`-v`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor--v)[`--verbose`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---verbose)

Use verbose output. May be specified twice for “very verbose” output which
includes extra output such as dependency warnings and build script output.
May also be specified with the `term.verbose` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`-q`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor--q)[`--quiet`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---quiet)

Do not print cargo log messages.
May also be specified with the `term.quiet` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--color` _when_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---color)

Control when colored output is used. Valid values:

- `auto` (default): Automatically detect if color support is available on the
terminal.
- `always`: Always display colors.
- `never`: Never display colors.

May also be specified with the `term.color` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

### Common Options

[`+` _toolchain_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor-+toolchain)

If Cargo has been installed with rustup, and the first argument to `cargo`
begins with `+`, it will be interpreted as a rustup toolchain name (such
as `+stable` or `+nightly`).
See the [rustup documentation](https://rust-lang.github.io/rustup/overrides.html)
for more information about how toolchain overrides work.

[`--config` _KEY=VALUE_ or _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---config)

Overrides a Cargo configuration value. The argument should be in TOML syntax of `KEY=VALUE`,
or provided as a path to an extra configuration file. This flag may be specified multiple times.
See the [command-line overrides section](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-overrides) for more information.

[`-C` _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor--C)

Changes the current working directory before executing any specified operations. This affects
things like where cargo looks by default for the project manifest (`Cargo.toml`), as well as
the directories searched for discovering `.cargo/config.toml`, for example. This option must
appear before the command name, for example `cargo -C path/to/my-project build`.

This option is only available on the [nightly\\
channel](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/appendix-07-nightly-rust.html) and
requires the `-Z unstable-options` flag to enable (see
[#10098](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/10098)).

[`-h`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor--h)[`--help`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor---help)

Prints help information.

[`-Z` _flag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-vendor--Z)

Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo. Run `cargo -Z help` for details.

## ENVIRONMENT

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#environment-variables-1) for
details on environment variables that Cargo reads.

## EXIT STATUS

- `0`: Cargo succeeded.
- `101`: Cargo failed to complete.

## EXAMPLES

1. Vendor all dependencies into a local “vendor” folder


```

cargo vendor
```

2. Vendor all dependencies into a local “third-party/vendor” folder


```

cargo vendor third-party/vendor
```

3. Vendor the current workspace as well as another to “vendor”


```

cargo vendor -s ../path/to/Cargo.toml
```

4. Vendor and redirect the necessary vendor configs to a config file.


```

cargo vendor > path/to/my/cargo/config.toml
```


## SEE ALSO

[cargo(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo1)

# Package Commands

- [cargo init](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-init1)
- [cargo install](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-install1)
- [cargo new](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-new1)
- [cargo search](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-search1)
- [cargo uninstall](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-uninstall1)

# cargo-init(1)

## NAME

cargo-init — Create a new Cargo package in an existing directory

## SYNOPSIS

`cargo init` \[ _options_\] \[ _path_\]

## DESCRIPTION

This command will create a new Cargo manifest in the current directory. Give a
path as an argument to create in the given directory.

If there are typically-named Rust source files already in the directory, those
will be used. If not, then a sample `src/main.rs` file will be created, or
`src/lib.rs` if `--lib` is passed.

If the directory is not already in a VCS repository, then a new repository
is created (see `--vcs` below).

See [cargo-new(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-new1) for a similar command which will create a new package in
a new directory.

## OPTIONS

### Init Options

[`--bin`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---bin)

Create a package with a binary target (`src/main.rs`).
This is the default behavior.

[`--lib`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---lib)

Create a package with a library target (`src/lib.rs`).

[`--edition` _edition_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---edition)

Specify the Rust edition to use. Default is 2024.
Possible values: 2015, 2018, 2021, 2024

[`--name` _name_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---name)

Set the package name. Defaults to the directory name.

[`--vcs` _vcs_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---vcs)

Initialize a new VCS repository for the given version control system (git,
hg, pijul, or fossil) or do not initialize any version control at all
(none). If not specified, defaults to `git` or the configuration value
`cargo-new.vcs`, or `none` if already inside a VCS repository.

[`--registry` _registry_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---registry)

This sets the `publish` field in `Cargo.toml` to the given registry name
which will restrict publishing only to that registry.

Registry names are defined in [Cargo config files](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).
If not specified, the default registry defined by the `registry.default`
config key is used. If the default registry is not set and `--registry` is not
used, the `publish` field will not be set which means that publishing will not
be restricted.

### Display Options

[`-v`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init--v)[`--verbose`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---verbose)

Use verbose output. May be specified twice for “very verbose” output which
includes extra output such as dependency warnings and build script output.
May also be specified with the `term.verbose` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`-q`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init--q)[`--quiet`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---quiet)

Do not print cargo log messages.
May also be specified with the `term.quiet` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--color` _when_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---color)

Control when colored output is used. Valid values:

- `auto` (default): Automatically detect if color support is available on the
terminal.
- `always`: Always display colors.
- `never`: Never display colors.

May also be specified with the `term.color` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

### Common Options

[`+` _toolchain_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init-+toolchain)

If Cargo has been installed with rustup, and the first argument to `cargo`
begins with `+`, it will be interpreted as a rustup toolchain name (such
as `+stable` or `+nightly`).
See the [rustup documentation](https://rust-lang.github.io/rustup/overrides.html)
for more information about how toolchain overrides work.

[`--config` _KEY=VALUE_ or _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---config)

Overrides a Cargo configuration value. The argument should be in TOML syntax of `KEY=VALUE`,
or provided as a path to an extra configuration file. This flag may be specified multiple times.
See the [command-line overrides section](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-overrides) for more information.

[`-C` _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init--C)

Changes the current working directory before executing any specified operations. This affects
things like where cargo looks by default for the project manifest (`Cargo.toml`), as well as
the directories searched for discovering `.cargo/config.toml`, for example. This option must
appear before the command name, for example `cargo -C path/to/my-project build`.

This option is only available on the [nightly\\
channel](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/appendix-07-nightly-rust.html) and
requires the `-Z unstable-options` flag to enable (see
[#10098](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/10098)).

[`-h`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init--h)[`--help`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init---help)

Prints help information.

[`-Z` _flag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-init--Z)

Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo. Run `cargo -Z help` for details.

## ENVIRONMENT

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#environment-variables-1) for
details on environment variables that Cargo reads.

## EXIT STATUS

- `0`: Cargo succeeded.
- `101`: Cargo failed to complete.

## EXAMPLES

1. Create a binary Cargo package in the current directory:


```

cargo init
```


## SEE ALSO

[cargo(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo1), [cargo-new(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-new1)

# cargo-install(1)

## NAME

cargo-install — Build and install a Rust binary

## SYNOPSIS

`cargo install` \[ _options_\] _crate_\[@ _version_\]…

`cargo install` \[ _options_\] `--path` _path_

`cargo install` \[ _options_\] `--git` _url_ \[ _crate_…\]

`cargo install` \[ _options_\] `--list`

## DESCRIPTION

This command manages Cargo’s local set of installed binary crates. Only
packages which have executable `[[bin]]` or `[[example]]` targets can be
installed, and all executables are installed into the installation root’s
`bin` folder. By default only binaries, not examples, are installed.

The installation root is determined, in order of precedence:

- `--root` option
- `CARGO_INSTALL_ROOT` environment variable
- `install.root` Cargo [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1)
- `CARGO_HOME` environment variable
- `$HOME/.cargo`

There are multiple sources from which a crate can be installed. The default
source location is crates.io but the `--git`, `--path`, and `--registry` flags
can change this source. If the source contains more than one package (such as
crates.io or a git repository with multiple crates) the _crate_ argument is
required to indicate which crate should be installed.

Crates from crates.io can optionally specify the version they wish to install
via the `--version` flags, and similarly packages from git repositories can
optionally specify the branch, tag, or revision that should be installed. If a
crate has multiple binaries, the `--bin` argument can selectively install only
one of them, and if you’d rather install examples the `--example` argument can
be used as well.

If the package is already installed, Cargo will reinstall it if the installed
version does not appear to be up-to-date. If any of the following values
change, then Cargo will reinstall the package:

- The package version and source.
- The set of binary names installed.
- The chosen features.
- The profile (`--profile`).
- The target (`--target`).

Installing with `--path` will always build and install, unless there are
conflicting binaries from another package. The `--force` flag may be used to
force Cargo to always reinstall the package.

If the source is crates.io or `--git` then by default the crate will be built
in a temporary target directory. To avoid this, the target directory can be
specified by setting the `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` environment variable to a
path. In particular, this can be useful for caching build artifacts on
continuous integration systems.

### Dealing with the Lockfile

By default, the `Cargo.lock` file that is included with the package will be
ignored. This means that Cargo will recompute which versions of dependencies
to use, possibly using newer versions that have been released since the
package was published. The `--locked` flag can be used to force Cargo to use
the packaged `Cargo.lock` file if it is available. This may be useful for
ensuring reproducible builds, to use the exact same set of dependencies that
were available when the package was published. It may also be useful if a
newer version of a dependency is published that no longer builds on your
system, or has other problems. The downside to using `--locked` is that you
will not receive any fixes or updates to any dependency. Note that Cargo did
not start publishing `Cargo.lock` files until version 1.37, which means
packages published with prior versions will not have a `Cargo.lock` file
available.

### Configuration Discovery

This command operates on system or user level, not project level.
This means that the local [configuration discovery](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#hierarchical-structure) is ignored.
Instead, the configuration discovery begins at `$CARGO_HOME/config.toml`.
If the package is installed with `--path $PATH`, the local configuration
will be used, beginning discovery at `$PATH/.cargo/config.toml`.

## OPTIONS

### Install Options

[`--vers` _version_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---vers)[`--version` _version_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---version)

Specify a version to install. This may be a [version\\
requirement](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#specifying-dependencies), like `~1.2`, to have Cargo
select the newest version from the given requirement. If the version does not
have a requirement operator (such as `^` or `~`), then it must be in the form
_MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH_, and will install exactly that version; it is _not_
treated as a caret requirement like Cargo dependencies are.

[`--git` _url_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---git)

Git URL to install the specified crate from.

[`--branch` _branch_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---branch)

Branch to use when installing from git.

[`--tag` _tag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---tag)

Tag to use when installing from git.

[`--rev` _sha_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---rev)

Specific commit to use when installing from git.

[`--path` _path_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---path)

Filesystem path to local crate to install from.

[`--list`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---list)

List all installed packages and their versions.

[`-n`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install--n)[`--dry-run`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---dry-run)

(unstable) Perform all checks without installing.

[`-f`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install--f)[`--force`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---force)

Force overwriting existing crates or binaries. This can be used if a package
has installed a binary with the same name as another package. This is also
useful if something has changed on the system that you want to rebuild with,
such as a newer version of `rustc`.

[`--no-track`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---no-track)

By default, Cargo keeps track of the installed packages with a metadata file
stored in the installation root directory. This flag tells Cargo not to use or
create that file. With this flag, Cargo will refuse to overwrite any existing
files unless the `--force` flag is used. This also disables Cargo’s ability to
protect against multiple concurrent invocations of Cargo installing at the
same time.

[`--bin` _name_…](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---bin)

Install only the specified binary.

[`--bins`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---bins)

Install all binaries. This is the default behavior.

[`--example` _name_…](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---example)

Install only the specified example.

[`--examples`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---examples)

Install all examples.

[`--root` _dir_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---root)

Directory to install packages into.

[`--registry` _registry_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---registry)

Name of the registry to use. Registry names are defined in [Cargo config\\
files](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1). If not specified, the default registry is used,
which is defined by the `registry.default` config key which defaults to
`crates-io`.

[`--index` _index_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---index)

The URL of the registry index to use.

### Feature Selection

The feature flags allow you to control which features are enabled. When no
feature options are given, the `default` feature is activated for every
selected package.

See [the features documentation](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-feature-options)
for more details.

[`-F` _features_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install--F)[`--features` _features_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---features)

Space or comma separated list of features to activate. Features of workspace
members may be enabled with `package-name/feature-name` syntax. This flag may
be specified multiple times, which enables all specified features.

[`--all-features`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---all-features)

Activate all available features of all selected packages.

[`--no-default-features`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---no-default-features)

Do not activate the `default` feature of the selected packages.

### Compilation Options

[`--target` _triple_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---target)

Install for the specified target architecture. The default is the host architecture. The general format of the triple is
`<arch><sub>-<vendor>-<sys>-<abi>`.

Possible values:

- Any supported target in `rustc --print target-list`.
- `"host-tuple"`, which will internally be substituted by the host’s target. This can be particularly useful if you’re cross-compiling some crates, and don’t want to specify your host’s machine as a target (for instance, an `xtask` in a shared project that may be worked on by many hosts).
- A path to a custom target specification. See [Custom Target Lookup Path](https://doc.rust-lang.org/rustc/targets/custom.html#custom-target-lookup-path) for more information.

This may also be specified with the `build.target` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

Note that specifying this flag makes Cargo run in a different mode where the
target artifacts are placed in a separate directory. See the
[build cache](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#build-cache) documentation for more details.

[`--target-dir` _directory_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---target-dir)

Directory for all generated artifacts and intermediate files. May also be
specified with the `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` environment variable, or the
`build.target-dir` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).
Defaults to a new temporary folder located in the
temporary directory of the platform.

When using `--path`, by default it will use `target` directory in the workspace
of the local crate unless `--target-dir`
is specified.

[`--debug`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---debug)

Build with the `dev` profile instead of the `release` profile.
See also the `--profile` option for choosing a specific profile by name.

[`--profile` _name_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---profile)

Install with the given profile.
See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#profiles) for more details on profiles.

[`--timings`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---timings)

Output information how long each compilation takes, and track concurrency
information over time.

A file `cargo-timing.html` will be written to the `target/cargo-timings`
directory at the end of the build. An additional report with a timestamp
in its filename is also written if you want to look at a previous run.
These reports are suitable for human consumption only, and do not provide
machine-readable timing data.

### Manifest Options

[`--ignore-rust-version`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---ignore-rust-version)

Ignore `rust-version` specification in packages.

[`--locked`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---locked)

Asserts that the exact same dependencies and versions are used as when the
existing `Cargo.lock` file was originally generated. Cargo will exit with an
error when either of the following scenarios arises:

- The lock file is missing.
- Cargo attempted to change the lock file due to a different dependency resolution.

It may be used in environments where deterministic builds are desired,
such as in CI pipelines.

[`--offline`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---offline)

Prevents Cargo from accessing the network for any reason. Without this
flag, Cargo will stop with an error if it needs to access the network and
the network is not available. With this flag, Cargo will attempt to
proceed without the network if possible.

Beware that this may result in different dependency resolution than online
mode. Cargo will restrict itself to crates that are downloaded locally, even
if there might be a newer version as indicated in the local copy of the index.
See the [cargo-fetch(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-fetch1) command to download dependencies before going
offline.

May also be specified with the `net.offline` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--frozen`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---frozen)

Equivalent to specifying both `--locked` and `--offline`.

### Miscellaneous Options

[`-j` _N_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install--j)[`--jobs` _N_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---jobs)

Number of parallel jobs to run. May also be specified with the
`build.jobs` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1). Defaults to
the number of logical CPUs. If negative, it sets the maximum number of
parallel jobs to the number of logical CPUs plus provided value. If
a string `default` is provided, it sets the value back to defaults.
Should not be 0.

[`--keep-going`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---keep-going)

Build as many crates in the dependency graph as possible, rather than aborting
the build on the first one that fails to build.

For example if the current package depends on dependencies `fails` and `works`,
one of which fails to build, `cargo install -j1` may or may not build the
one that succeeds (depending on which one of the two builds Cargo picked to run
first), whereas `cargo install -j1 --keep-going` would definitely run both
builds, even if the one run first fails.

### Display Options

[`-v`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install--v)[`--verbose`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---verbose)

Use verbose output. May be specified twice for “very verbose” output which
includes extra output such as dependency warnings and build script output.
May also be specified with the `term.verbose` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`-q`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install--q)[`--quiet`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---quiet)

Do not print cargo log messages.
May also be specified with the `term.quiet` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--color` _when_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---color)

Control when colored output is used. Valid values:

- `auto` (default): Automatically detect if color support is available on the
terminal.
- `always`: Always display colors.
- `never`: Never display colors.

May also be specified with the `term.color` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--message-format` _fmt_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---message-format)

The output format for diagnostic messages. Can be specified multiple times
and consists of comma-separated values. Valid values:

- `human` (default): Display in a human-readable text format. Conflicts with
`short` and `json`.
- `short`: Emit shorter, human-readable text messages. Conflicts with `human`
and `json`.
- `json`: Emit JSON messages to stdout. See
[the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#json-messages)
for more details. Conflicts with `human` and `short`.
- `json-diagnostic-short`: Ensure the `rendered` field of JSON messages contains
the “short” rendering from rustc. Cannot be used with `human` or `short`.
- `json-diagnostic-rendered-ansi`: Ensure the `rendered` field of JSON messages
contains embedded ANSI color codes for respecting rustc’s default color
scheme. Cannot be used with `human` or `short`.
- `json-render-diagnostics`: Instruct Cargo to not include rustc diagnostics
in JSON messages printed, but instead Cargo itself should render the
JSON diagnostics coming from rustc. Cargo’s own JSON diagnostics and others
coming from rustc are still emitted. Cannot be used with `human` or `short`.

### Common Options

[`+` _toolchain_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install-+toolchain)

If Cargo has been installed with rustup, and the first argument to `cargo`
begins with `+`, it will be interpreted as a rustup toolchain name (such
as `+stable` or `+nightly`).
See the [rustup documentation](https://rust-lang.github.io/rustup/overrides.html)
for more information about how toolchain overrides work.

[`--config` _KEY=VALUE_ or _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---config)

Overrides a Cargo configuration value. The argument should be in TOML syntax of `KEY=VALUE`,
or provided as a path to an extra configuration file. This flag may be specified multiple times.
See the [command-line overrides section](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-overrides) for more information.

[`-C` _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install--C)

Changes the current working directory before executing any specified operations. This affects
things like where cargo looks by default for the project manifest (`Cargo.toml`), as well as
the directories searched for discovering `.cargo/config.toml`, for example. This option must
appear before the command name, for example `cargo -C path/to/my-project build`.

This option is only available on the [nightly\\
channel](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/appendix-07-nightly-rust.html) and
requires the `-Z unstable-options` flag to enable (see
[#10098](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/10098)).

[`-h`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install--h)[`--help`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install---help)

Prints help information.

[`-Z` _flag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-install--Z)

Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo. Run `cargo -Z help` for details.

## ENVIRONMENT

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#environment-variables-1) for
details on environment variables that Cargo reads.

## EXIT STATUS

- `0`: Cargo succeeded.
- `101`: Cargo failed to complete.

## EXAMPLES

1. Install or upgrade a package from crates.io:


```

cargo install ripgrep
```

2. Install or reinstall the package in the current directory:


```

cargo install --path .
```

3. View the list of installed packages:


```

cargo install --list
```


## SEE ALSO

[cargo(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo1), [cargo-uninstall(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-uninstall1), [cargo-search(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-search1), [cargo-publish(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-publish1)

# cargo-new(1)

## NAME

cargo-new — Create a new Cargo package

## SYNOPSIS

`cargo new` \[ _options_\] _path_

## DESCRIPTION

This command will create a new Cargo package in the given directory. This
includes a simple template with a `Cargo.toml` manifest, sample source file,
and a VCS ignore file. If the directory is not already in a VCS repository,
then a new repository is created (see `--vcs` below).

See [cargo-init(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-init1) for a similar command which will create a new manifest
in an existing directory.

## OPTIONS

### New Options

[`--bin`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---bin)

Create a package with a binary target (`src/main.rs`).
This is the default behavior.

[`--lib`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---lib)

Create a package with a library target (`src/lib.rs`).

[`--edition` _edition_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---edition)

Specify the Rust edition to use. Default is 2024.
Possible values: 2015, 2018, 2021, 2024

[`--name` _name_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---name)

Set the package name. Defaults to the directory name.

[`--vcs` _vcs_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---vcs)

Initialize a new VCS repository for the given version control system (git,
hg, pijul, or fossil) or do not initialize any version control at all
(none). If not specified, defaults to `git` or the configuration value
`cargo-new.vcs`, or `none` if already inside a VCS repository.

[`--registry` _registry_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---registry)

This sets the `publish` field in `Cargo.toml` to the given registry name
which will restrict publishing only to that registry.

Registry names are defined in [Cargo config files](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).
If not specified, the default registry defined by the `registry.default`
config key is used. If the default registry is not set and `--registry` is not
used, the `publish` field will not be set which means that publishing will not
be restricted.

### Display Options

[`-v`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new--v)[`--verbose`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---verbose)

Use verbose output. May be specified twice for “very verbose” output which
includes extra output such as dependency warnings and build script output.
May also be specified with the `term.verbose` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`-q`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new--q)[`--quiet`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---quiet)

Do not print cargo log messages.
May also be specified with the `term.quiet` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--color` _when_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---color)

Control when colored output is used. Valid values:

- `auto` (default): Automatically detect if color support is available on the
terminal.
- `always`: Always display colors.
- `never`: Never display colors.

May also be specified with the `term.color` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

### Common Options

[`+` _toolchain_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new-+toolchain)

If Cargo has been installed with rustup, and the first argument to `cargo`
begins with `+`, it will be interpreted as a rustup toolchain name (such
as `+stable` or `+nightly`).
See the [rustup documentation](https://rust-lang.github.io/rustup/overrides.html)
for more information about how toolchain overrides work.

[`--config` _KEY=VALUE_ or _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---config)

Overrides a Cargo configuration value. The argument should be in TOML syntax of `KEY=VALUE`,
or provided as a path to an extra configuration file. This flag may be specified multiple times.
See the [command-line overrides section](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-overrides) for more information.

[`-C` _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new--C)

Changes the current working directory before executing any specified operations. This affects
things like where cargo looks by default for the project manifest (`Cargo.toml`), as well as
the directories searched for discovering `.cargo/config.toml`, for example. This option must
appear before the command name, for example `cargo -C path/to/my-project build`.

This option is only available on the [nightly\\
channel](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/appendix-07-nightly-rust.html) and
requires the `-Z unstable-options` flag to enable (see
[#10098](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/10098)).

[`-h`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new--h)[`--help`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new---help)

Prints help information.

[`-Z` _flag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-new--Z)

Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo. Run `cargo -Z help` for details.

## ENVIRONMENT

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#environment-variables-1) for
details on environment variables that Cargo reads.

## EXIT STATUS

- `0`: Cargo succeeded.
- `101`: Cargo failed to complete.

## EXAMPLES

1. Create a binary Cargo package in the given directory:


```

cargo new foo
```


## SEE ALSO

[cargo(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo1), [cargo-init(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-init1)

# cargo-search(1)

## NAME

cargo-search — Search packages in the registry. Default registry is crates.io

## SYNOPSIS

`cargo search` \[ _options_\] \[ _query_…\]

## DESCRIPTION

This performs a textual search for crates on [https://crates.io](https://crates.io/). The matching
crates will be displayed along with their description in TOML format suitable
for copying into a `Cargo.toml` manifest.

## OPTIONS

### Search Options

[`--limit` _limit_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search---limit)

Limit the number of results (default: 10, max: 100).

[`--index` _index_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search---index)

The URL of the registry index to use.

[`--registry` _registry_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search---registry)

Name of the registry to use. Registry names are defined in [Cargo config\\
files](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1). If not specified, the default registry is used,
which is defined by the `registry.default` config key which defaults to
`crates-io`.

### Display Options

[`-v`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search--v)[`--verbose`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search---verbose)

Use verbose output. May be specified twice for “very verbose” output which
includes extra output such as dependency warnings and build script output.
May also be specified with the `term.verbose` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`-q`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search--q)[`--quiet`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search---quiet)

Do not print cargo log messages.
May also be specified with the `term.quiet` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--color` _when_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search---color)

Control when colored output is used. Valid values:

- `auto` (default): Automatically detect if color support is available on the
terminal.
- `always`: Always display colors.
- `never`: Never display colors.

May also be specified with the `term.color` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

### Common Options

[`+` _toolchain_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search-+toolchain)

If Cargo has been installed with rustup, and the first argument to `cargo`
begins with `+`, it will be interpreted as a rustup toolchain name (such
as `+stable` or `+nightly`).
See the [rustup documentation](https://rust-lang.github.io/rustup/overrides.html)
for more information about how toolchain overrides work.

[`--config` _KEY=VALUE_ or _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search---config)

Overrides a Cargo configuration value. The argument should be in TOML syntax of `KEY=VALUE`,
or provided as a path to an extra configuration file. This flag may be specified multiple times.
See the [command-line overrides section](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-overrides) for more information.

[`-C` _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search--C)

Changes the current working directory before executing any specified operations. This affects
things like where cargo looks by default for the project manifest (`Cargo.toml`), as well as
the directories searched for discovering `.cargo/config.toml`, for example. This option must
appear before the command name, for example `cargo -C path/to/my-project build`.

This option is only available on the [nightly\\
channel](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/appendix-07-nightly-rust.html) and
requires the `-Z unstable-options` flag to enable (see
[#10098](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/10098)).

[`-h`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search--h)[`--help`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search---help)

Prints help information.

[`-Z` _flag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-search--Z)

Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo. Run `cargo -Z help` for details.

## ENVIRONMENT

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#environment-variables-1) for
details on environment variables that Cargo reads.

## EXIT STATUS

- `0`: Cargo succeeded.
- `101`: Cargo failed to complete.

## EXAMPLES

1. Search for a package from crates.io:


```

cargo search serde
```


## SEE ALSO

[cargo(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo1), [cargo-install(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-install1), [cargo-publish(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-publish1)

# cargo-uninstall(1)

## NAME

cargo-uninstall — Remove a Rust binary

## SYNOPSIS

`cargo uninstall` \[ _options_\] \[ _spec_…\]

## DESCRIPTION

This command removes a package installed with [cargo-install(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-install1). The _spec_
argument is a package ID specification of the package to remove (see
[cargo-pkgid(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-pkgid1)).

By default all binaries are removed for a crate but the `--bin` and
`--example` flags can be used to only remove particular binaries.

The installation root is determined, in order of precedence:

- `--root` option
- `CARGO_INSTALL_ROOT` environment variable
- `install.root` Cargo [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1)
- `CARGO_HOME` environment variable
- `$HOME/.cargo`

## OPTIONS

### Uninstall Options

[`-p`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall--p)[`--package` _spec_…](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall---package)

Package to uninstall.

[`--bin` _name_…](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall---bin)

Only uninstall the binary _name_.

[`--root` _dir_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall---root)

Directory to uninstall packages from.

### Display Options

[`-v`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall--v)[`--verbose`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall---verbose)

Use verbose output. May be specified twice for “very verbose” output which
includes extra output such as dependency warnings and build script output.
May also be specified with the `term.verbose` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`-q`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall--q)[`--quiet`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall---quiet)

Do not print cargo log messages.
May also be specified with the `term.quiet` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--color` _when_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall---color)

Control when colored output is used. Valid values:

- `auto` (default): Automatically detect if color support is available on the
terminal.
- `always`: Always display colors.
- `never`: Never display colors.

May also be specified with the `term.color` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

### Common Options

[`+` _toolchain_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall-+toolchain)

If Cargo has been installed with rustup, and the first argument to `cargo`
begins with `+`, it will be interpreted as a rustup toolchain name (such
as `+stable` or `+nightly`).
See the [rustup documentation](https://rust-lang.github.io/rustup/overrides.html)
for more information about how toolchain overrides work.

[`--config` _KEY=VALUE_ or _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall---config)

Overrides a Cargo configuration value. The argument should be in TOML syntax of `KEY=VALUE`,
or provided as a path to an extra configuration file. This flag may be specified multiple times.
See the [command-line overrides section](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-overrides) for more information.

[`-C` _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall--C)

Changes the current working directory before executing any specified operations. This affects
things like where cargo looks by default for the project manifest (`Cargo.toml`), as well as
the directories searched for discovering `.cargo/config.toml`, for example. This option must
appear before the command name, for example `cargo -C path/to/my-project build`.

This option is only available on the [nightly\\
channel](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/appendix-07-nightly-rust.html) and
requires the `-Z unstable-options` flag to enable (see
[#10098](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/10098)).

[`-h`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall--h)[`--help`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall---help)

Prints help information.

[`-Z` _flag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-uninstall--Z)

Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo. Run `cargo -Z help` for details.

## ENVIRONMENT

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#environment-variables-1) for
details on environment variables that Cargo reads.

## EXIT STATUS

- `0`: Cargo succeeded.
- `101`: Cargo failed to complete.

## EXAMPLES

1. Uninstall a previously installed package.


```

cargo uninstall ripgrep
```


## SEE ALSO

[cargo(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo1), [cargo-install(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-install1)

# cargo-login(1)

## NAME

cargo-login — Log in to a registry

## SYNOPSIS

`cargo login` \[ _options_\] \[`--` _args_\]

## DESCRIPTION

This command will run a credential provider to save a token so that commands
that require authentication, such as [cargo-publish(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-publish1), will be
automatically authenticated.

All the arguments following the two dashes (`--`) are passed to the credential provider.

For the default `cargo:token` credential provider, the token is saved
in `$CARGO_HOME/credentials.toml`. `CARGO_HOME` defaults to `.cargo`
in your home directory.

If a registry has a credential-provider specified, it will be used. Otherwise,
the providers from the config value `registry.global-credential-providers` will
be attempted, starting from the end of the list.

The _token_ will be read from stdin.

The API token for crates.io may be retrieved from [https://crates.io/me](https://crates.io/me).

Take care to keep the token secret, it should not be shared with anyone else.

## OPTIONS

### Login Options

[`--registry` _registry_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login---registry)

Name of the registry to use. Registry names are defined in [Cargo config\\
files](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1). If not specified, the default registry is used,
which is defined by the `registry.default` config key which defaults to
`crates-io`.

### Display Options

[`-v`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login--v)[`--verbose`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login---verbose)

Use verbose output. May be specified twice for “very verbose” output which
includes extra output such as dependency warnings and build script output.
May also be specified with the `term.verbose` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`-q`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login--q)[`--quiet`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login---quiet)

Do not print cargo log messages.
May also be specified with the `term.quiet` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--color` _when_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login---color)

Control when colored output is used. Valid values:

- `auto` (default): Automatically detect if color support is available on the
terminal.
- `always`: Always display colors.
- `never`: Never display colors.

May also be specified with the `term.color` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

### Common Options

[`+` _toolchain_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login-+toolchain)

If Cargo has been installed with rustup, and the first argument to `cargo`
begins with `+`, it will be interpreted as a rustup toolchain name (such
as `+stable` or `+nightly`).
See the [rustup documentation](https://rust-lang.github.io/rustup/overrides.html)
for more information about how toolchain overrides work.

[`--config` _KEY=VALUE_ or _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login---config)

Overrides a Cargo configuration value. The argument should be in TOML syntax of `KEY=VALUE`,
or provided as a path to an extra configuration file. This flag may be specified multiple times.
See the [command-line overrides section](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-overrides) for more information.

[`-C` _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login--C)

Changes the current working directory before executing any specified operations. This affects
things like where cargo looks by default for the project manifest (`Cargo.toml`), as well as
the directories searched for discovering `.cargo/config.toml`, for example. This option must
appear before the command name, for example `cargo -C path/to/my-project build`.

This option is only available on the [nightly\\
channel](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/appendix-07-nightly-rust.html) and
requires the `-Z unstable-options` flag to enable (see
[#10098](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/10098)).

[`-h`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login--h)[`--help`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login---help)

Prints help information.

[`-Z` _flag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-login--Z)

Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo. Run `cargo -Z help` for details.

## ENVIRONMENT

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#environment-variables-1) for
details on environment variables that Cargo reads.

## EXIT STATUS

- `0`: Cargo succeeded.
- `101`: Cargo failed to complete.

## EXAMPLES

1. Save the token for the default registry:


```

cargo login
```

2. Save the token for a specific registry:


```

cargo login --registry my-registry
```


## SEE ALSO

[cargo(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo1), [cargo-logout(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-logout1), [cargo-publish(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-publish1)

# cargo-logout(1)

## NAME

cargo-logout — Remove an API token from the registry locally

## SYNOPSIS

`cargo logout` \[ _options_\]

## DESCRIPTION

This command will run a credential provider to remove a saved token.

For the default `cargo:token` credential provider, credentials are stored
in `$CARGO_HOME/credentials.toml` where `$CARGO_HOME` defaults to `.cargo`
in your home directory.

If a registry has a credential-provider specified, it will be used. Otherwise,
the providers from the config value `registry.global-credential-providers` will
be attempted, starting from the end of the list.

If `--registry` is not specified, then the credentials for the default
registry will be removed (configured by
[`registry.default`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#registrydefault), which defaults
to [https://crates.io/](https://crates.io/)).

This will not revoke the token on the server. If you need to revoke the token,
visit the registry website and follow its instructions (see
[https://crates.io/me](https://crates.io/me) to revoke the token for [https://crates.io/](https://crates.io/)).

## OPTIONS

### Logout Options

[`--registry` _registry_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout---registry)

Name of the registry to use. Registry names are defined in [Cargo config\\
files](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1). If not specified, the default registry is used,
which is defined by the `registry.default` config key which defaults to
`crates-io`.

### Display Options

[`-v`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout--v)[`--verbose`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout---verbose)

Use verbose output. May be specified twice for “very verbose” output which
includes extra output such as dependency warnings and build script output.
May also be specified with the `term.verbose` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`-q`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout--q)[`--quiet`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout---quiet)

Do not print cargo log messages.
May also be specified with the `term.quiet` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--color` _when_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout---color)

Control when colored output is used. Valid values:

- `auto` (default): Automatically detect if color support is available on the
terminal.
- `always`: Always display colors.
- `never`: Never display colors.

May also be specified with the `term.color` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

### Common Options

[`+` _toolchain_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout-+toolchain)

If Cargo has been installed with rustup, and the first argument to `cargo`
begins with `+`, it will be interpreted as a rustup toolchain name (such
as `+stable` or `+nightly`).
See the [rustup documentation](https://rust-lang.github.io/rustup/overrides.html)
for more information about how toolchain overrides work.

[`--config` _KEY=VALUE_ or _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout---config)

Overrides a Cargo configuration value. The argument should be in TOML syntax of `KEY=VALUE`,
or provided as a path to an extra configuration file. This flag may be specified multiple times.
See the [command-line overrides section](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-overrides) for more information.

[`-C` _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout--C)

Changes the current working directory before executing any specified operations. This affects
things like where cargo looks by default for the project manifest (`Cargo.toml`), as well as
the directories searched for discovering `.cargo/config.toml`, for example. This option must
appear before the command name, for example `cargo -C path/to/my-project build`.

This option is only available on the [nightly\\
channel](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/appendix-07-nightly-rust.html) and
requires the `-Z unstable-options` flag to enable (see
[#10098](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/10098)).

[`-h`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout--h)[`--help`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout---help)

Prints help information.

[`-Z` _flag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-logout--Z)

Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo. Run `cargo -Z help` for details.

## ENVIRONMENT

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#environment-variables-1) for
details on environment variables that Cargo reads.

## EXIT STATUS

- `0`: Cargo succeeded.
- `101`: Cargo failed to complete.

## EXAMPLES

1. Remove the default registry token:


```

cargo logout
```

2. Remove the token for a specific registry:


```

cargo logout --registry my-registry
```


## SEE ALSO

[cargo(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo1), [cargo-login(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-login1)

# cargo-owner(1)

## NAME

cargo-owner — Manage the owners of a crate on the registry

## SYNOPSIS

`cargo owner` \[ _options_\] `--add` _login_ \[ _crate_\]

`cargo owner` \[ _options_\] `--remove` _login_ \[ _crate_\]

`cargo owner` \[ _options_\] `--list` \[ _crate_\]

## DESCRIPTION

This command will modify the owners for a crate on the registry. Owners of a
crate can upload new versions and yank old versions. Non-team owners can also
modify the set of owners, so take care!

This command requires you to be authenticated with either the `--token` option
or using [cargo-login(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-login1).

If the crate name is not specified, it will use the package name from the
current directory.

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-owner) for more
information about owners and publishing.

## OPTIONS

### Owner Options

[`-a`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner--a)[`--add` _login_…](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---add)

Invite the given user or team as an owner.

[`-r`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner--r)[`--remove` _login_…](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---remove)

Remove the given user or team as an owner.

[`-l`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner--l)[`--list`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---list)

List owners of a crate.

[`--token` _token_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---token)

API token to use when authenticating. This overrides the token stored in
the credentials file (which is created by [cargo-login(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-login1)).

[Cargo config](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1) environment variables can be
used to override the tokens stored in the credentials file. The token for
crates.io may be specified with the `CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN` environment
variable. Tokens for other registries may be specified with environment
variables of the form `CARGO_REGISTRIES_NAME_TOKEN` where `NAME` is the name
of the registry in all capital letters.

[`--index` _index_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---index)

The URL of the registry index to use.

[`--registry` _registry_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---registry)

Name of the registry to use. Registry names are defined in [Cargo config\\
files](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1). If not specified, the default registry is used,
which is defined by the `registry.default` config key which defaults to
`crates-io`.

### Display Options

[`-v`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner--v)[`--verbose`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---verbose)

Use verbose output. May be specified twice for “very verbose” output which
includes extra output such as dependency warnings and build script output.
May also be specified with the `term.verbose` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`-q`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner--q)[`--quiet`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---quiet)

Do not print cargo log messages.
May also be specified with the `term.quiet` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--color` _when_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---color)

Control when colored output is used. Valid values:

- `auto` (default): Automatically detect if color support is available on the
terminal.
- `always`: Always display colors.
- `never`: Never display colors.

May also be specified with the `term.color` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

### Common Options

[`+` _toolchain_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner-+toolchain)

If Cargo has been installed with rustup, and the first argument to `cargo`
begins with `+`, it will be interpreted as a rustup toolchain name (such
as `+stable` or `+nightly`).
See the [rustup documentation](https://rust-lang.github.io/rustup/overrides.html)
for more information about how toolchain overrides work.

[`--config` _KEY=VALUE_ or _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---config)

Overrides a Cargo configuration value. The argument should be in TOML syntax of `KEY=VALUE`,
or provided as a path to an extra configuration file. This flag may be specified multiple times.
See the [command-line overrides section](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-overrides) for more information.

[`-C` _PATH_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner--C)

Changes the current working directory before executing any specified operations. This affects
things like where cargo looks by default for the project manifest (`Cargo.toml`), as well as
the directories searched for discovering `.cargo/config.toml`, for example. This option must
appear before the command name, for example `cargo -C path/to/my-project build`.

This option is only available on the [nightly\\
channel](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/appendix-07-nightly-rust.html) and
requires the `-Z unstable-options` flag to enable (see
[#10098](https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/10098)).

[`-h`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner--h)[`--help`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner---help)

Prints help information.

[`-Z` _flag_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-owner--Z)

Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo. Run `cargo -Z help` for details.

## ENVIRONMENT

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#environment-variables-1) for
details on environment variables that Cargo reads.

## EXIT STATUS

- `0`: Cargo succeeded.
- `101`: Cargo failed to complete.

## EXAMPLES

1. List owners of a package:


```

cargo owner --list foo
```

2. Invite an owner to a package:


```

cargo owner --add username foo
```

3. Remove an owner from a package:


```

cargo owner --remove username foo
```


## SEE ALSO

[cargo(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo1), [cargo-login(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-login1), [cargo-publish(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-publish1)

# cargo-package(1)

## NAME

cargo-package — Assemble the local package into a distributable tarball

## SYNOPSIS

`cargo package` \[ _options_\]

## DESCRIPTION

This command will create a distributable, compressed `.crate` file with the
source code of the package in the current directory. The resulting file will be
stored in the `target/package` directory. This performs the following steps:

1. Load and check the current workspace, performing some basic checks.
   - Path dependencies are not allowed unless they have a version key. Cargo
     will ignore the path key for dependencies in published packages.
     `dev-dependencies` do not have this restriction.
2. Create the compressed `.crate` file.
   - The original `Cargo.toml` file is rewritten and normalized.
   - `[patch]`, `[replace]`, and `[workspace]` sections are removed from the
     manifest.
   - `Cargo.lock` is always included. When missing, a new lock file will be
     generated unless the `--exclude-lockfile` flag is used. [cargo-install(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-install1)
     will use the packaged lock file if the `--locked` flag is used.
   - A `.cargo_vcs_info.json` file is included that contains information
     about the current VCS checkout hash if available, as well as a flag if the
     worktree is dirty.
   - Symlinks are flattened to their target files.
   - Files and directories are included or excluded based on rules mentioned in
     [the `[include]` and `[exclude]` fields](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#the-exclude-and-include-fields).
3. Extract the `.crate` file and build it to verify it can build.
   - This will rebuild your package from scratch to ensure that it can be
     built from a pristine state. The `--no-verify` flag can be used to skip
     this step.
4. Check that build scripts did not modify any source files.


The list of files included can be controlled with the `include` and `exclude`
fields in the manifest.

See [the reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#publishing-on-cratesio) for more details about
packaging and publishing.

### .cargo\_vcs\_info.json format

Will generate a `.cargo_vcs_info.json` in the following format

```javascript

{
 "git": {
   "sha1": "aac20b6e7e543e6dd4118b246c77225e3a3a1302",
   "dirty": true
 },
 "path_in_vcs": ""
}
```

`dirty` indicates that the Git worktree was dirty when the package
was built.

`path_in_vcs` will be set to a repo-relative path for packages
in subdirectories of the version control repository.

The compatibility of this file is maintained under the same policy
as the JSON output of [cargo-metadata(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-metadata1).

Note that this file provides a best-effort snapshot of the VCS information.
However, the provenance of the package is not verified.
There is no guarantee that the source code in the tarball matches the VCS information.

## OPTIONS

### Package Options

[`-l`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package--l)[`--list`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---list)

Print files included in a package without making one.

[`--no-verify`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---no-verify)

Don’t verify the contents by building them.

[`--no-metadata`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---no-metadata)

Ignore warnings about a lack of human-usable metadata (such as the description
or the license).

[`--allow-dirty`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---allow-dirty)

Allow working directories with uncommitted VCS changes to be packaged.

[`--exclude-lockfile`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---exclude-lockfile)

Don’t include the lock file when packaging.

This flag is not for general use.
Some tools may expect a lock file to be present (e.g. `cargo install --locked`).
Consider other options before using this.

[`--index` _index_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---index)

The URL of the registry index to use.

[`--registry` _registry_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---registry)

Name of the registry to package for; see `cargo publish --help` for more details
about configuration of registry names. The packages will not be published
to this registry, but if we are packaging multiple inter-dependent crates,
lock-files will be generated under the assumption that dependencies will be
published to this registry.

[`--message-format` _fmt_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---message-format)

Specifies the output message format.
Currently, it only works with `--list` and affects the file listing format.
This is unstable and requires `-Zunstable-options`.
Valid output formats:

- `human` (default): Display in a file-per-line format.
- `json`: Emit machine-readable JSON information about each package.
One package per JSON line (Newline delimited JSON).


```javascript

{
    /* The Package ID Spec of the package. */
    "id": "path+file:///home/foo#0.0.0",
    /* Files of this package */
    "files" {
      /* Relative path in the archive file. */
      "Cargo.toml.orig": {
        /* Where the file is from.
         - "generate" for file being generated during packaging
         - "copy" for file being copied from another location.
      */
      "kind": "copy",
      /* For the "copy" kind,
         it is an absolute path to the actual file content.
         For the "generate" kind,
         it is the original file the generated one is based on.
      */
      "path": "/home/foo/Cargo.toml"
    },
    "Cargo.toml": {
      "kind": "generate",
      "path": "/home/foo/Cargo.toml"
    },
    "src/main.rs": {
      "kind": "copy",
      "path": "/home/foo/src/main.rs"
    }
}
}
```

### Package Selection

By default, when no package selection options are given, the packages selected
depend on the selected manifest file (based on the current working directory if
`--manifest-path` is not given). If the manifest is the root of a workspace then
the workspaces default members are selected, otherwise only the package defined
by the manifest will be selected.

The default members of a workspace can be set explicitly with the
`workspace.default-members` key in the root manifest. If this is not set, a
virtual workspace will include all workspace members (equivalent to passing
`--workspace`), and a non-virtual workspace will include only the root crate itself.

[`-p` _spec_…](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package--p)[`--package` _spec_…](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---package)

Package only the specified packages. See [cargo-pkgid(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-pkgid1) for the
SPEC format. This flag may be specified multiple times and supports common Unix
glob patterns like `*`, `?` and `[]`. However, to avoid your shell accidentally
expanding glob patterns before Cargo handles them, you must use single quotes or
double quotes around each pattern.

[`--workspace`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---workspace)

Package all members in the workspace.

[`--exclude` _SPEC_…](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---exclude)

Exclude the specified packages. Must be used in conjunction with the
`--workspace` flag. This flag may be specified multiple times and supports
common Unix glob patterns like `*`, `?` and `[]`. However, to avoid your shell
accidentally expanding glob patterns before Cargo handles them, you must use
single quotes or double quotes around each pattern.

### Compilation Options

[`--target` _triple_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---target)

Package for the specified target architecture. Flag may be specified multiple times. The default is the host architecture. The general format of the triple is
`<arch><sub>-<vendor>-<sys>-<abi>`.

Possible values:

- Any supported target in `rustc --print target-list`.
- `"host-tuple"`, which will internally be substituted by the host’s target. This can be particularly useful if you’re cross-compiling some crates, and don’t want to specify your host’s machine as a target (for instance, an `xtask` in a shared project that may be worked on by many hosts).
- A path to a custom target specification. See [Custom Target Lookup Path](https://doc.rust-lang.org/rustc/targets/custom.html#custom-target-lookup-path) for more information.

This may also be specified with the `build.target` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

Note that specifying this flag makes Cargo run in a different mode where the
target artifacts are placed in a separate directory. See the
[build cache](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#build-cache) documentation for more details.

[`--target-dir` _directory_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---target-dir)

Directory for all generated artifacts and intermediate files. May also be
specified with the `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` environment variable, or the
`build.target-dir` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).
Defaults to `target` in the root of the workspace.

### Feature Selection

The feature flags allow you to control which features are enabled. When no
feature options are given, the `default` feature is activated for every
selected package.

See [the features documentation](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#command-line-feature-options)
for more details.

[`-F` _features_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package--F)[`--features` _features_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---features)

Space or comma separated list of features to activate. Features of workspace
members may be enabled with `package-name/feature-name` syntax. This flag may
be specified multiple times, which enables all specified features.

[`--all-features`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---all-features)

Activate all available features of all selected packages.

[`--no-default-features`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---no-default-features)

Do not activate the `default` feature of the selected packages.

### Manifest Options

[`--manifest-path` _path_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---manifest-path)

Path to the `Cargo.toml` file. By default, Cargo searches for the
`Cargo.toml` file in the current directory or any parent directory.

[`--locked`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---locked)

Asserts that the exact same dependencies and versions are used as when the
existing `Cargo.lock` file was originally generated. Cargo will exit with an
error when either of the following scenarios arises:

- The lock file is missing.
- Cargo attempted to change the lock file due to a different dependency resolution.

It may be used in environments where deterministic builds are desired,
such as in CI pipelines.

[`--offline`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---offline)

Prevents Cargo from accessing the network for any reason. Without this
flag, Cargo will stop with an error if it needs to access the network and
the network is not available. With this flag, Cargo will attempt to
proceed without the network if possible.

Beware that this may result in different dependency resolution than online
mode. Cargo will restrict itself to crates that are downloaded locally, even
if there might be a newer version as indicated in the local copy of the index.
See the [cargo-fetch(1)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#cargo-fetch1) command to download dependencies before going
offline.

May also be specified with the `net.offline` [config value](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#configuration-1).

[`--frozen`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---frozen)

Equivalent to specifying both `--locked` and `--offline`.

### Miscellaneous Options

[`-j` _N_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package--j)[`--jobs` _N_](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/print.html#option-cargo-package---jobs)
